questions: Does the annexe have its own completely separate external entrance — i.e. can the occupant reach the annexe without passing through the main house at all?
Are all the annexe rooms (living/kitchen, bedroom, shower room) on a single ground level with no internal stairs?
What broadband provider does the vendor use and approximately what speeds do they get — particularly upload?
What are the approximate annual oil heating costs for the main house? When was the Worcester boiler last serviced and is there a service record?
What are the approximate annual LPG costs for the annexe? How is the LPG supplied — bottles or a fixed tank — and where is it located on the plot?
Is there an onward chain on the vendor's side?
Can the building control indemnity policy be obtained as a condition prior to exchange, rather than after survey? Our solicitor will need this confirmed early.
What is the approximate size of the paddock in acres? Is it currently grazed or maintained, and is there any form of pedestrian access to it?
red_flags: BC indemnity: vendor says 'after survey if needed' — this must be a pre-exchange condition; instruct solicitor to require it unconditionally
LPG annexe heating: confirm tank/bottle location and annual cost; budget for possible ASHP upgrade longer term
Broadband: vendor works from home 'without issues' but speeds unconfirmed — check Ofcom checker at TA20 3HG before viewing

We would like to arrange a viewing — what dates are available?

scores.label: Annexe status
scores.note: Gym foundations BUILT for annexe. 10.8m × 4.4m = 47.5m². Comfortably fits: double bedroom (4×3.5m), shower room (2×2m), open-plan kitchen/living (4×4.4m) with space to spare. No planning required. Solar + mains electric. ✓

scores.label: Ground floor bedroom
scores.note: Outbuilding — single storey, all ground floor. 47.5m² total. ✓

scores.label: Fuel type
scores.note: Solar primary + mains electric backup. Heat pump for main house. Gym on main house electric circuit. ✓

scores.label: Drainage type
scores.note: Mains drainage confirmed — BUT currently routed via neighbour's property. Seller pays £50/month. Quote of £3k to reroute direct. Needs clarification before exchange.

scores.label: Solar / Heat pump
scores.note: Solar panels primary power source + mains backup. Heat pump confirmed. Gym powered from main house circuit. ✓

questions: The water and sewage connection runs via the neighbour's property — can we get written confirmation from the neighbour that this arrangement is permanent and will transfer with the sale? Has it been formalised in any legal agreement (easement or deed)?
The £3,000 quote to reroute the water/sewage direct — is the seller open to completing this before exchange (or reducing the price by £3,000 to allow us to do it)?

The gym runs off the main house electric circuit — is there a separate sub-board or fuse in the gym, and what is the supply capacity (i.e. enough for an electric shower and appliances simultaneously)?
Can you confirm the gym has a shower room or wet room, or is there currently no shower at all?
The agent mentioned no planning required — can the solicitor confirm this via the Swansea planning portal before exchange? Has any certificate of lawful use been issued?
Is there level or ramped access from the main house to the gym — no steps — suitable for a person with limited mobility?
What broadband speeds are available at SA6? We'd appreciate if you could check next time you visit.
We would like to arrange a viewing — what dates are available?
red_flags: Water/sewage via neighbour's property — must have written legal agreement transferring with sale before exchange. If not in place, budget £3k to reroute direct.
No shower or kitchen in gym yet — plumbing feasible (water available) but fit-out cost needed (estimate £5k–10k for shower room + kitchenette)
Broadband unknown — check Ofcom for SA6 5QL
Planning confirmation recommended — agent says no planning needed but solicitor should verify via planning search

questions: The property flooded about 11 years ago and the garden still floods after prolonged rain — what is the current EA flood risk designation for DT2 7QL, and can the vendor provide details of the river defence system that was installed?

The heating is a back boiler via the log burner with immersion electric for hot water — there is no gas or oil central heating. What is the approximate annual electricity and wood fuel cost? Is the back boiler in good working order and when was it last serviced?
For the garage conversion: what are the dimensions of the garage, and does it have an existing solid floor, electricity supply and roof in usable condition?
Is there any scope to add a downstairs WC or shower room to the main house — and if so, where would be the most practical location?
We would like to arrange a viewing — what dates are available?
red_flags: FLOODED 11 years ago — garden still floods in prolonged rain. Check current EA flood designation for DT2 7QL.
Back boiler via log burner — unusual heating system, expensive and unreliable. No gas available in village. Budget for ASHP replacement.
No bathroom or shower downstairs at all — serious mobility barrier without major works
No confirmed annexe — garage conversion needed; planning required; garage condition poor ('needs TLC')
Onward chain
